name: Zoom API
what_it_does: Zoom's REST API for creating and managing meetings, webinars, recordings, and participants programmatically to automate video communication workflows.
use_cases: Scheduling and creating Zoom meetings from agents
Managing webinar registrations and attendees at scale
Retrieving meeting recordings and transcripts automatically
Building calendar integration agents that schedule video calls
Monitoring meeting usage and generating attendance reports
not_for: Custom video streaming outside Zoom infrastructure
Very small teams without Zoom subscriptions
best_when: You need to automate scheduling, management, and reporting for video meetings within an organization already using Zoom.
avoid_when: Your team uses Google Meet or Teams primarily, or you need in-call real-time processing.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for user-context apps. Server-to-Server OAuth for automated agents (JWT deprecated in 2023). Scopes per resource type.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Zoom account: 40-min group meetings, basic API access
pricing.notes: API access requires marketplace app creation. Server-to-Server OAuth apps available on any plan. Webinar API requires Webinar add-on.

performance.rate_limits: 10 requests/second; daily limits by API type

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON errors with code and message. Some error codes require cross-referencing documentation to understand.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Meeting creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT app type was deprecated June 2023 - migrate to Server-to-Server OAuth for agent use
Meeting join URLs expire and are single-use for registration-required meetings
Recording download URLs expire after 24 hours - agents must download promptly
Webhook event verification required via webhook secret token validation
Account-level rate limits shared across all apps - multiple agents can exhaust quota
